value.reason: The page does document MCP tool governance/allow-listing as a shipped feature: the `mcp` action with `mcp(server/tool)`, `mcp(server/*)`, or `mcp(*)` syntax is clearly described in the permissions table, supporting the concept of controlling which MCP tools an agent may use. However, two issues exist. First, the label "Per-Tool MCP Permission" is not the provider's real name for this feature — the page calls it the `mcp` action/permission within a broader "Agent Permissions" system (product: Antigravity 2.0), not "Per-Tool MCP Permission." Second, the description claims this is configured "per-agent by specifying the MCP server and the exact tool name to permit," which is a partial/misleading characterization: the page shows three syntactic forms — `mcp(server/tool)` (exact tool), `mcp(server/*)` (all tools on a server), and `mcp(*)` (all tools everywhere) — evaluated across Deny/Ask/Allow lists, not simply an allow-list of named tools per agent. The description omits the Deny and Ask modes entirely and incorrectly implies only exact tool-name grants exist, when wildcard grants are equally supported and prominent.
